findings.evidence: `rg` shows `SemigroupGroupTimeAxis.lean:77/82/86/91` have the same signatures as `SemigroupGroupTimeSlice.lean:76/82/86/92`, e.g. both prove `(hF : IsSemigroupGroupPD F) (t : ℝ≥0) : 0 ≤ F (t, 0)`.
findings.fix: Remove these duplicate declarations, or move the shared `F (t, 0)` facts to a common lower-level location and have both the time-slice and time-axis APIs derive/alias from that single source.
findings.issue: `timeAxis_nonneg`, `timeAxis_im`, `timeAxis_re_nonneg`, and `timeAxis_eq_ofReal_re` restate the existing fixed-time diagonal API for the same term `F (t, 0)`, creating parallel names for identical facts rather than using the more general slice theorem.

summary: The kernel and one-variable positive-definite statements are at the intended time-axis level, but the zero-spatial value API duplicates existing fixed-time diagonal lemmas with the same signatures.
